data.title: First Digital's February Reserve Account Report Shows Total Reserve Assets Exceed $2.05 Billion
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: First Digital's February reserve account report shows that First Digital's total reserve assets are US$2,051,348,188.70, and the FDUSD issuance (circulation) is 2,041,924,819.94 pieces. The reserve assets cover more than 100% of the issued FDUSD, meeting the 1:1 reserve requirements.

data.title: He Yi: Binance has its own risk control, and my personal judgment does not represent FDUSD’s
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Binance co-founder He Yi wrote: "No one knew that Justin Sun would tweet in the middle of the night, but Binance launched Launchpool's stablecoin and will regularly check the funds. Binance has its own risk control. Binance postlisting is also looking for fdusd to try to understand the situation. We are just making logical judgments. There is no large investor group or insider group. Many people are asking if Binance launchpool's FDUSD will have any problems. Sisi (Binance customer service) casually replied to my judgment in a group. My judgment does not represent FDUSD company."

data.title: X Corp. Wants Supreme Court to Stop 'Suspicionless' Crypto Transaction Data Requests
data.description:
The amicus curiae brief aims to prevent law enforcement from using subpoenas to get user data from third parties without warrants.
source: https://www.bankless.com/read/x-corp-wants-supreme-court-to-stop-suspicionless-crypto-transaction-data-requests

data.title: Trump Media shares fall after POTUS kicks off sale of $2.3bn stake
data.description:
Shares of Trump Media & Technology Group (TMTG) nosedived on Wednesday after a regulatory filing revealed plans for a large stock sale, including the potential sale of US President Donald Trump’s $2. 3 billion stake. The update comes against the backdrop of Trump’s upcoming announcement of new trade tariffs, which he has branded as “liberation day” […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-media-shares-fall-2-3bn-stake-sale/

data.title: Cathie Wood's fund ARKK increased its holdings of Robinhood stocks by more than 100,000 shares
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reported that according to Ark Invest Daily, Cathie Wood's fund ARKK increased its holdings of 103,202 shares of Robinhood (HOOD) on April 1. The fund currently holds more than 10.5 million shares of Robinhood, worth approximately US$178.6 million.

data.title: Crypto PAC Fairshake Backs Two Crypto-Friendly Candidates to Win Florida Congressional By-elections
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Randy Fine and Jimmy Patronis, two Republican candidates supported by Fairshake, a super PAC of the US crypto industry, both won the Florida congressional by-election, consolidating the Republican Party's slim majority in the House of Representatives. Both publicly support digital assets and innovative policies, and Fairshake's organizations have invested more than $1.5 million in advertising support for them. (CoinDesk)

data.title: First Digital: Sun Yuchen's accusations against FDT are false, and FDUSD reserves are safe and reliable
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: FDUSD issuer First Digital posted on the X platform that Sun Yuchen's accusations against First Digital Trust are false. The dispute is related to TUSD, not FDUSD. First Digital is fully solvent, and FDUSD reserves are safe and reliable, and are backed by US Treasury bonds. The exact ISIN numbers of all FDUSD reserves are listed in its certification report and are clearly recorded.
First Digital added that this is a typical defamation action by Sun Yuchen aimed at attacking competitors of his business, and FDT will take legal action to protect its rights and reputation.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Holders Face Increased IRS Scrutiny in 2025
data.description:
According to CoinDesk, cryptocurrency holders are experiencing heightened scrutiny from the IRS in 2025, with audits on the rise. The evolving tax regulations mean that even minor oversights can lead to significant penalties or costly audits. As the IRS intensifies its focus on cryptocurrency transactions, investors must be vigilant in their reporting practices to avoid potential pitfalls.

One common mistake is neglecting wallet-based accounting. The IRS now requires detailed reporting of each wallet's transactions and balances, eliminating the practice of consolidating all trades on a single spreadsheet. Whether using hot wallets, cold wallets, or a combination, each wallet's records must be individually tracked. Tools such as CoinTracking, CoinLedger, or TaxBit can assist in syncing real-time data from various exchanges, ensuring compliance and preventing surprises during IRS audits.

Another frequent error involves misreporting staking rewards. These rewards are considered taxable income as soon as they are received, regardless of whether they have been sold for fiat currency. Many investors mistakenly believe they only need to report staking income upon sale, but the IRS requires reporting at the time of receipt. For example, earning 2 ETH worth $3,000 in staking rewards constitutes taxable income when received. Failing to accurately report these amounts can attract unwanted attention from regulators closely monitoring crypto activities.

Additionally, overlooking IRS letters and Form 1099-DA can lead to complications. Notices such as Notice 6371, Notice 6374, and CP2000 may be issued if discrepancies are found in tax filings. In 2025, crypto exchanges will also issue Form 1099-DA, detailing crypto income, trades, and rewards. Any inconsistency between this form and reported information is a clear red flag. It is crucial to review these documents for accuracy and address any errors promptly to prevent escalation.

Failing to report all transactions is another pitfall. The IRS and its partners possess advanced blockchain analysis tools capable of tracking activity on decentralized exchanges (DEXs) and privacy coins. Every transaction, including trades, airdrops, forks, and rewards, must be included in tax filings. Unreported transactions linked to wallet addresses can lead to significant issues.

Lastly, the 2025 tax year offers an opportunity to adjust the crypto cost basis under new guidelines. Investors can reallocate unused cost basis across wallets or exchange accounts, provided they document the method before their first 2025 trade and adhere to specific record-keeping requirements. This can reduce capital gains tax liability. However, excessive deductions, such as inflating business expenses or hobby-related costs, can trigger audits if deemed unrealistic. The IRS scrutinizes deductions that do not align with typical income levels, so maintaining reasonable claims and thorough supporting records is essential.

To remain audit-ready, cryptocurrency investors should utilize reliable crypto tax software, meticulously review their returns, maintain comprehensive records, and be transparent about any past errors. A proactive approach ensures preparedness for IRS inquiries and allows investors to focus on their crypto investments.

data.title: Wintermute is the largest FDUSD holding entity outside of Binance, with approximately 65.46 million tokens.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Arkham monitoring data, Wintermute is the largest FDUSD holding entity besides Binance, and currently holds approximately 65.46 million tokens, accounting for approximately 2.5% of the total circulation.
